title: Spicy Festive Slovakian Chorizo Pierogi
description: A lacto-vegetarian take on traditional Slovakian pierogi, featuring spicy chorizo and crispy fried onions, elevated with a flambé twist.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Make a well in the center and add eggs, sour cream, and shredded cheese. Knead the dough until it becomes smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for at least 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 2 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin sheet, about 1/16 inch thick.
Use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough. Gather the scraps, re-roll the dough, and repeat.
In a large skillet, cook chorizo sausage over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until browned and crispy, about 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine cooked chorizo, green onions, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Place a tablespoon of chorizo mixture onto the center of each dough circle, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the dough over the filling, forming a half-circle shape, and press the edges together to seal the pierogi.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
Fry the pierogi in batches until they are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Drain the pierogi on paper towels and sprinkle with chopped green onions.
To flambé, carefully add a tablespoon of apple cider vinegar to the skillet and ignite it, using a long match or lighter to toast the onions until caramelized, about 1 minute.
Serve the pierogi hot, topped with flambéed onions and chopped fresh parsley (optional).
